diary: I woke up in Sammamish just before 6:30, coffee going and the morning light already feeling warm—one of those early-June Mondays where the city seems to hum a little louder. I did a quick check of drive vs ferry options and pointed my car toward Bothell. By the time I got to the turbine staging area, the air had that bright, clean bite and I was glad for the steady routine: inspections, paperwork, the usual “listen to what the gear is saying” kind of work. After lunch at MOD Pizza downtown Bothell, I drove up toward Seattle for the USA vs Wales match. The Kraken Bar opened early, and it felt like downtown collectively leaned in—until I could finally relax my shoulders. Afterward, I swung by Safeway in Bellevue to restock without going wild. Evening was a vinyl rabbit hole at home, then I ended the day at Lake Union Park, kayaking at sunset with the water calm and the ferry times just… background fun. I’m tired, but in a good way—like the day actually fit together.
thought_process: I chose to drive toward Bothell first because the early logistics looked simpler than ferry hopping, and with turbine work you can’t afford delays or last-minute reroutes. I stuck to inspections and paperwork in the morning because that’s when my focus is sharp and the site conditions are usually easiest to read. The MOD Pizza lunch decision was mostly practicality—fast, cold drink, keep energy up without losing momentum. When I headed downtown for the USA vs Wales match, it wasn’t just the game; it was the atmosphere—bars open early, people already out, and it helped me decompress after a technical stretch of the day. The light rail closure made me plan parking and timing a bit differently, and I felt slightly more “hands-on” about routes. By late afternoon my mood softened, and the kayak at Lake Union pushed it into calm.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
